meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Freelancers: Overcome Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Learn to leverage SaaS pricing models to overcome project stagnation. A high-impact, intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for developers.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing ideas and start building systems. By implementing a value-based SaaS pricing model, you shift your focus from 'what to build' to 'who to serve.' Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to refine your pricing logic, turning abstract concepts into high-margin, automated revenue streams for clients.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a blinking cursor—the silent, mocking heartbeat of the 'no project ideas' trap. You’ve mastered the frameworks, but you’re stuck in a loop of building portfolio pieces that never see a dime. The friction isn't in your code; it's in your lack of a commercial engine.

Real profitability doesn't come from a clever algorithm; it comes from a pricing model that scales while you sleep. By treating SaaS pricing as the core architectural constraint, you force your projects into high-value niches. We aren't just building apps today; we are building businesses that clients pay to own.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Niche
steps.description: Identify a high-friction business process. Instead of building a generic tool, use Antigravity to map out a workflow that saves a client at least 5 hours per week.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the workflow of a boutique marketing agency. Identify 3 repetitive tasks suitable for a micro-SaaS. Generate a feature set that justifies a $99/month subscription.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research potential SaaS niches for boutique agencies and summarize the top 3 high-value problems.'

steps.title: Architect the Pricing Logic
steps.description: Build a flexible pricing model that allows for tiered subscriptions. This ensures you can scale from MVP to enterprise features without rewriting your database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a SaaS pricing engine. Include fields for tiers, feature flags, usage-based limits, and Stripe integration webhooks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement a Stripe-compatible billing service using TypeScript and Zod for validation.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Infrastructure
steps.description: Use your stack to deploy the foundational project. Focus on speed and security, ensuring the pricing logic is decoupled from the UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 14 project with authentication, Tailwind CSS, and a billing dashboard. Ensure the code is modular and ready for Stripe implementation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a new Next.js project with modular components for the billing dashboard.'

steps.title: Implement Automated Deployment
steps.description: Ensure your project is instantly deployable. Freelancers win when they can show a live demo in minutes, not days.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file that includes environment variables for Stripe API keys and database connections.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure Vercel build settings for the repository and automate environment variable injection.'

steps.title: Stress Test the Pricing Logic
steps.description: Simulate subscription upgrades and cancellations to ensure the logic handles edge cases without breaking the user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of unit tests for the billing logic, covering user subscription upgrades, failed payments, and feature flag enforcement.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run unit tests on the billing service to verify edge cases for subscription tiers.'

pro_tips: Always price based on the client's time saved, not your hours logged.
Use feature flags to gate functionality; it makes A/B testing pricing tiers trivial.
Never hardcode pricing; pull subscription data from your database via an environment-aware service.
Automate your customer onboarding email sequence the moment a user hits the checkout page.
Offer an 'Annual' discount to improve cash flow and reduce churn—it’s the fastest way to stabilize a micro-SaaS.
content.next_steps: After completing this, deploy your MVP to a staging environment and reach out to three potential beta users in your target niche to validate the pricing tier.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop building 'projects' and start building revenue-generating assets. Take this logic, apply it to your next client brief, and stop guessing at value. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
